metadata: Gary, Indiana - Wikipedia content overview
content: Gary, Indiana - Wikipedia content overview This dataset contains textual content from Wikipedia about Gary, Indiana, a city located in Lake County. With a population of 69,093 as of the 2020 census, it ranks as Indiana's eleventh-most populous city. Established in 1906 as a company town for U.S. Steel, Gary has a rich industrial history, notably housing the largest steel mill complex in North America. However, the city has faced significant population decline due to the downturn of the steel industry since the 1970s. The city is recognized for its cultural diversity, notably having one of the highest percentages of African Americans in the nation.

metadata: Wikipedia content on the Holland Land Company
content: Wikipedia content on the Holland Land Company This dataset contains text from a Wikipedia article about the Holland Land Company, an unincorporated syndicate of Dutch investors based in Amsterdam, which acquired significant tracts of land in America for development between 1789 and 1858. The company's major acquisition was the western portion of the Phelps and Gorham Purchase, known as the Holland Purchase. Due to restrictions on land ownership for aliens in New York, the investors relied on trustees to manage purchases. The syndicate aimed to profit from rapid land sales but had to invest in infrastructure to attract settlers.

Compiled by the City Planning Department, it consists of four historical datasets on building permits, criminal arrests, public assistance, and community facilities, reflecting conditions in 1972. Maintained by the Digital Scholarship Services of the University of Pittsburgh, the dataset aims to provide insights into urban development and community conditions during that period. metadata: Metadata for Automated Traffic Enforcement dataset.
content: Metadata for Automated Traffic Enforcement dataset. This dataset contains metadata for the Automated Traffic Enforcement (ATE) initiative by the District of Columbia. The ATE uses photo enforcement cameras to promote traffic law compliance and improve safety on DC streets. The dataset has been accessed over 6,500 times and is available in tabular format.

metadata: Washington D.C. Charter Schools Web Map Service Data
content: Washington D.C. Charter Schools Web Map Service Data This dataset contains Web Map Service (WMS) information for Washington D.C. public charter schools, including school attendance zones for senior high schools from the 2015-16 to 2022-23 school years. The data is geospatially referenced using multiple coordinate reference systems (CRS) and includes various image formats. The contribution from the D.C. Office of the Chief Technology Officer emphasizes the new school attendance boundaries, which were established by the Advisory Committee on Student Assignment and accepted by the Mayor, effective from the 2015-16 school year. This dataset is updated for the 2018-2019 school year.

metadata: EAARL Coastal Topography Data - Assateague Island, 2010
content: EAARL Coastal Topography Data - Assateague Island, 2010 This dataset consists of a digital elevation model (DEM) created from lidar elevation measurements of Assateague Island National Seashore in Maryland and Virginia. Collected by the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) and the National Park Service (NPS) on March 19 and 24, 2010, the data features high-resolution topographical measurements obtained using the Experimental Advanced Airborne Research Lidar (EAARL). The dataset includes critical information on ground elevation and vegetation canopy, with a laser swath of approximately 240 meters and average point spacing of 2-3 meters. For more details, refer to USGS Data Series 628.

metadata: EAARL Topography Data of George Washington Birthplace National Monument
content: EAARL Topography Data of George Washington Birthplace National Monument This dataset contains a bare earth elevation map, also known as a Digital Elevation Model (DEM), for the George Washington Birthplace National Monument. Created through remote-sensing techniques, the data was collected using the NASA Experimental Advanced Airborne Research Lidar (EAARL). This advanced system gathers high-resolution elevation measurements to analyze ground elevation, vegetation canopy, and coastal topography. The dataset is part of a collaborative effort involving the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), NASA, and the National Park Service (NPS), providing valuable insights into the monument's topographical features.

metadata: EAARL Topography Raw Data for GEWA
content: EAARL Topography Raw Data for GEWA This dataset contains raw topographic data from the EAARL (Airborne Lidar) specific to the George Washington Birthplace National Monument. It includes Lidar DEM GeoTIFFs, hillshade grids, and contour shapefiles organized by UTM coordinates. Each GIS layer is accompanied by an XML metadata file. The dataset adheres to the UTM Zone 18 N Coordinate System, NAD83 Datum, and GRS80 Spheroid. Furthermore, it provides essential vector data, including coastlines and park boundaries, used in creating PDF maps. Data quality is generally high, but any potential uncertainties should be assessed when analyzing results.

It categorizes deaths according to ICD-10 codes for unintentional, suicide, homicide, and undetermined intent. Data are sourced from the National Vital Statistics System and are crucial for understanding mortality trends related to drug use. The dataset includes demographic characteristics and is adjusted for population estimates based on the 2010 U.S. census. Note that some states may have low death rates due to pending cases or misclassifications.

metadata: NCHS Drug Poisoning Mortality Data by County (2003-2021)
content: NCHS Drug Poisoning Mortality Data by County (2003-2021) This dataset contains county-level mortality rates from drug poisoning in the United States, covering the years 2003 to 2021. It includes estimates generated using hierarchical Bayesian models with spatial and temporal random effects, providing a detailed overview of drug overdose death rates across different counties. The data is intended for public health analysis and visualization, with associated dashboards depicting heat maps, grids, and trend-lines of mortality rates. The dataset originates from the NCHS Data Visualization Gallery, and some text may not be relevant to the primary data focus.
